leagueBriefing: Espanyol's frustrating winless run continued on Monday night as they were held to a 1-1 draw by basement-dwellers Real Oviedo at the RCDE Stadium.

games.headline: Kike García rescues point as Espanyol winless streak hits double digits
games.brief: Espanyol’s search for a spark continues as they played out a gritty 1-1 draw against a resilient Real Oviedo side. The visitors stunned the RCDE Stadium early when Alberto Reina found the back of the net just eight minutes in, capitalizing on a defensive lapse. While Kike García managed to restore parity before the break with a well-taken strike, the hosts couldn't find a winner despite a second-half onslaught. The result keeps Espanyol mired in a worrying slump, while Oviedo will take heart from a disciplined defensive performance that earned them a rare away point.
bullets: Alberto Reina gave the visitors a shock lead in the 8th minute, finishing an assist from Thiago Fernández.
Kike García equalized for the hosts in the 36th minute, converting a chance set up by Cyril Ngonge.
Espanyol dominated the stat sheet with 28 total shots and 58% possession but struggled with clinical finishing.
Oviedo goalkeeper Aarón Escandell was the hero for the visitors, making 8 saves to preserve the draw.
The draw extends Espanyol's winless run in LaLiga to 10 consecutive matches.
games.context: The draw leaves Espanyol stuck in mid-table mediocrity while Real Oviedo remains rooted to the bottom of the LaLiga standings.
